gaps.explanation: 「自信を持って導入された → しかし改善はほとんど現れなかった」という逆接。期待と結果の対比を示す nevertheless（それにもかかわらず）が適切。

gaps.explanation: 否定的な副詞句 Not until が文頭に出ると倒置が起こり、do/does/did + 主語 + 動詞原形の語順になる。よって did the team realize が正しい。

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Hidden Network Beneath the Forest
paragraphs: Walk into an old forest and it is easy to see the trees as separate individuals, each one quietly competing with its neighbours for light and water. For most of history, that is exactly how scientists described them. In recent decades, however, researchers digging into the soil have uncovered a far stranger and more cooperative picture.

Beneath the ground, the roots of many trees are linked by fine threads of fungus that stretch for miles. Through this living network, trees can pass sugar, water, and even chemical warnings from one to another. A tall, healthy tree may quietly feed a young seedling growing in its shade, while a tree under attack by insects can send signals that prepare its neighbours to defend themselves.

Some scientists warn that we should be careful not to imagine the forest as one big, friendly family. Trees still compete, and the fungi that connect them are taking payment in sugar for their services. Even so, the discovery has changed how we picture a forest: not as a crowd of strangers, but as a community bound together by threads we are only beginning to understand.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Trees are connected underground and can share resources and signals, so a forest works partly as a community.
Trees never compete with one another and always act as a single family.
The fungus in the soil is the only living thing that matters in a forest.
Scientists have fully explained everything about how forests work.

questions.before: Through the underground network, a tall, healthy tree may quietly
questions.after: a young seedling growing in its shade.

questions.prompt: 森を「一つの仲のよい家族」と単純に考えないよう注意している語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the roots of many trees are linked by fine threads of fungus
we should be careful not to imagine the forest as one big, friendly family
trees can pass sugar, water, and even chemical warnings
Walk into an old forest and it is easy to see the trees as separate individuals

questions.sentence: This exchange, however, is not always as generous as it sounds.

questions.prompt: 筆者が最後の段落で最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: A forest is best seen as a connected community, even though its trees still compete with one another.
Trees should be moved closer together so that they can share more sugar.
The discovery proves that forests have no competition of any kind.
Fungi are harmful and should be removed from forest soil.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Comeback of the Night Train
paragraphs: Not long ago, the night train seemed to belong to the past. Cheap flights had made it faster and often cheaper to fly across a continent, and many old sleeper services were quietly shut down. For a while it looked as though falling asleep in one city and waking up in another was a memory that would soon disappear.

Today, though, night trains are slowly returning to Europe. Growing concern about the pollution caused by flying has led many travellers to look for greener ways to move around. A night on the train means one less hotel to pay for, and passengers arrive rested in the heart of a city rather than at an airport far outside it. Several countries have begun to invest in new sleeper carriages and to reopen routes that were closed years ago.

Night trains are not perfect. Tickets can be more expensive than a budget flight, and a journey that takes two hours by plane may take twelve by rail. Yet for a growing number of people, the slower pace is part of the appeal. The night train offers something a plane cannot: the quiet pleasure of watching the world roll past your window as you travel gently towards sleep.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Night trains are becoming popular again, partly because they are greener and more comfortable than flying.
Night trains have completely disappeared and will never return.
Flying is always the best way to travel across a continent.
Night trains are cheaper than every other kind of travel.

questions.before: One reason night trains are returning is that many travellers now worry about the

questions.prompt: 夜行列車が飛行機にはない魅力を持つと述べている語句はどれですか。

spans.text: Cheap flights had made it faster and often cheaper to fly across a continent
Tickets can be more expensive than a budget flight
the quiet pleasure of watching the world roll past your window
many old sleeper services were quietly shut down

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Although night trains have some drawbacks, they are making a comeback as a greener and more relaxing way to travel.
Night trains are faster and cheaper than planes in every case.
People should stop travelling by train because it wastes time.
Airports are always located in the centre of the city.
